.svg-inline--fa {
  box-sizing: content-box;
  display: var(--fa-display, inline-block);
  height: 1em;
  overflow: visible;
  vertical-align: -0.125em;
  width: var(--fa-width, 1.25em);
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-2xs {
  vertical-align: 0.1em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-xs {
  vertical-align: 0em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-sm {
  vertical-align: -0.0714285714em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-lg {
  vertical-align: -0.2em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-xl {
  vertical-align: -0.25em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-2xl {
  vertical-align: -0.3125em;
}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-pull-left,
.svg-inline--fa .fa-pull-start {

}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-li {
  width: var(--fa-li-width, 2em);
  inset-inline-start: calc(-1 * var(--fa-li-width, 2em));
  inset-block-start: 0.25em; /* syncing vertical alignment with Web Font rendering */
}

.fa-layers-counter, .fa-layers-text {
  display: inline-block;
  position: absolute;
  text-align: center;
}

.fa-layers {
  display: inline-block;
  height: 1em;
  position: relative;
  text-align: center;
  vertical-align: -0.125em;
  width: var(--fa-width, 1.25em);
}
.fa-layers .svg-inline--fa {
  inset: 0;
  margin: auto;
  position: absolute;
  transform-origin: center center;
}

.fa-layers-text {
  left: 50%;
  top: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
  transform-origin: center center;
}

.fa-layers-counter {
  background-color: var(--fa-counter-background-color, #ff253a);
  border-radius: var(--fa-counter-border-radius, 1em);
  box-sizing: border-box;
  color: var(--fa-inverse, #fff);
  line-height: var(--fa-counter-line-height, 1);
  max-width: var(--fa-counter-max-width, 5em);
  min-width: var(--fa-counter-min-width, 1.5em);
  overflow: hidden;
  padding: var(--fa-counter-padding, 0.25em 0.5em);
  right: var(--fa-right, 0);
  text-overflow: ellipsis;
  top: var(--fa-top, 0);
  transform: scale(var(--fa-counter-scale, 0.25));
  transform-origin: top right;
}

.fa-layers-bottom-right {
  bottom: var(--fa-bottom, 0);
  right: var(--fa-right, 0);
  top: auto;
  transform: scale(var(--fa-layers-scale, 0.25));
  transform-origin: bottom right;
}

.fa-layers-bottom-left {
  bottom: var(--fa-bottom, 0);
  left: var(--fa-left, 0);
  right: auto;
  top: auto;
  transform: scale(var(--fa-layers-scale, 0.25));
  transform-origin: bottom left;
}

.fa-layers-top-right {
  top: var(--fa-top, 0);
  right: var(--fa-right, 0);
  transform: scale(var(--fa-layers-scale, 0.25));
  transform-origin: top right;
}

.fa-layers-top-left {
  left: var(--fa-left, 0);
  right: auto;
  top: var(--fa-top, 0);
  transform: scale(var(--fa-layers-scale, 0.25));
  transform-origin: top left;
}

.fa-1x {

.fa-2x {

.fa-3x {

.fa-2xs {
  font-size: calc(10 / 16 * 1em); /* converts a 10px size into an em-based value that's relative to the scale's 16px base */
  line-height: calc(1 / 10 * 1em); /* sets the line-height of the icon back to that of it's parent */

}

.fa-xs {
  font-size: calc(12 / 16 * 1em); /* converts a 12px size into an em-based value that's relative to the scale's 16px base */
  line-height: calc(1 / 12 * 1em); /* sets the line-height of the icon back to that of it's parent */
  vertical-align: calc((6 / 12 - 0.375) * 1em); /* vertically centers the icon taking into account the surrounding text's descender */
}

.fa-sm {

  line-height: calc(1 / 14 * 1em); /* sets the line-height of the icon back to that of it's parent */
  vertical-align: calc((6 / 14 - 0.375) * 1em); /* vertically centers the icon taking into account the surrounding text's descender */
}

.fa-lg {

}

.fa-xl {

.fa-2xl {

}

/* Notes:

*/
.fa-border {

}

.fa-pull-right,

}

.fa-beat {
  animation-name: fa-beat;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, ease-in-out);
}

.fa-bounce {
  animation-name: fa-bounce;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, cubic-bezier(0.28, 0.84, 0.42, 1));
}

.fa-fade {
  animation-name: fa-fade;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1));
}

.fa-beat-fade {
  animation-name: fa-beat-fade;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1));
}

.fa-flip {
  animation-name: fa-flip;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, ease-in-out);
}

.fa-shake {
  animation-name: fa-shake;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, linear);
}

.fa-spin {
  animation-name: fa-spin;
  animation-delay: var(--fa-animation-delay, 0s);
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 2s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, linear);
}

.fa-spin-reverse {
  --fa-animation-direction: reverse;
}

.fa-pulse,
.fa-spin-pulse {
  animation-name: fa-spin;
  animation-direction: var(--fa-animation-direction, normal);
  animation-duration: var(--fa-animation-duration, 1s);
  animation-iteration-count: var(--fa-animation-iteration-count, infinite);
  animation-timing-function: var(--fa-animation-timing, steps(8));
}

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  .fa-beat,
  .fa-bounce,
  .fa-fade,
  .fa-beat-fade,
  .fa-flip,
  .fa-pulse,
  .fa-shake,
  .fa-spin,
  .fa-spin-pulse {
    animation: none !important;
    transition: none !important;
  }
}
@keyframes fa-beat {
  0%, 90% {
    transform: scale(1);
  }
  45% {
    transform: scale(var(--fa-beat-scale, 1.25));
  }
}
@keyframes fa-bounce {
  0% {
    transform: scale(1, 1) translateY(0);
  }
  10% {
    transform: scale(var(--fa-bounce-start-scale-x, 1.1), var(--fa-bounce-start-scale-y, 0.9)) translateY(0);
  }
  30% {
    transform: scale(var(--fa-bounce-jump-scale-x, 0.9), var(--fa-bounce-jump-scale-y, 1.1)) translateY(var(--fa-bounce-height, -0.5em));
  }
  50% {
    transform: scale(var(--fa-bounce-land-scale-x, 1.05), var(--fa-bounce-land-scale-y, 0.95)) translateY(0);
  }
  57% {
    transform: scale(1, 1) translateY(var(--fa-bounce-rebound, -0.125em));
  }
  64% {
    transform: scale(1, 1) translateY(0);
  }
  100% {
    transform: scale(1, 1) translateY(0);
  }
}
@keyframes fa-fade {
  50% {
    opacity: var(--fa-fade-opacity, 0.4);
  }
}
@keyframes fa-beat-fade {
  0%, 100% {
    opacity: var(--fa-beat-fade-opacity, 0.4);
    transform: scale(1);
  }
  50% {
    opacity: 1;
    transform: scale(var(--fa-beat-fade-scale, 1.125));
  }
}
@keyframes fa-flip {
  50% {
    transform: rotate3d(var(--fa-flip-x, 0), var(--fa-flip-y, 1), var(--fa-flip-z, 0), var(--fa-flip-angle, -180deg));
  }
}
@keyframes fa-shake {
  0% {
    transform: rotate(-15deg);
  }
  4% {
    transform: rotate(15deg);
  }
  8%, 24% {
    transform: rotate(-18deg);
  }
  12%, 28% {
    transform: rotate(18deg);
  }
  16% {
    transform: rotate(-22deg);
  }
  20% {
    transform: rotate(22deg);
  }
  32% {
    transform: rotate(-12deg);
  }
  36% {
    transform: rotate(12deg);
  }
  40%, 100% {
    transform: rotate(0deg);
  }
}
@keyframes fa-spin {
  0% {
    transform: rotate(0deg);
  }
  100% {
    transform: rotate(360deg);
  }
}
.fa-rotate-90 {
  transform: rotate(90deg);
}

.fa-rotate-180 {
  transform: rotate(180deg);
}

.fa-rotate-270 {
  transform: rotate(270deg);
}

.fa-flip-horizontal {
  transform: scale(-1, 1);
}

.fa-flip-vertical {
  transform: scale(1, -1);
}

.fa-flip-both,
.fa-flip-horizontal.fa-flip-vertical {
  transform: scale(-1, -1);
}

.fa-rotate-by {
  transform: rotate(var(--fa-rotate-angle, 0));
}

.svg-inline--fa .fa-primary {
  fill: var(--fa-primary-color, currentColor);
  opacity: var(--fa-primary-opacity, 1);
}

.svg-inline--fa .fa-secondary {
  fill: var(--fa-secondary-color, currentColor);
  opacity: var(--fa-secondary-opacity, 0.4);
}

.svg-inline--fa.fa-swap-opacity .fa-primary {
  opacity: var(--fa-secondary-opacity, 0.4);
}

.svg-inline--fa.fa-swap-opacity .fa-secondary {
  opacity: var(--fa-primary-opacity, 1);
}

.svg-inline--fa mask .fa-primary,
.svg-inline--fa mask .fa-secondary {
  fill: black;
}

.svg-inline--fa.fa-inverse {
  fill: var(--fa-inverse, #fff);
}

.fa-stack {
  display: inline-block;
  height: 2em;
  line-height: 2em;
  position: relative;
  vertical-align: middle;
  width: 2.5em;
}

.fa-inverse {

.svg-inline--fa.fa-stack-1x {

}
.svg-inline--fa.fa-stack-2x {
  --fa-width: 2.5em;
  height: 2em;
  width: var(--fa-width);
}

.fa-stack-1x,
.fa-stack-2x {
  inset: 0;
  margin: auto;
  position: absolute;

<h1 id="fetching-dynamic-websites">Fetching dynamic websites<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#fetching-dynamic-websites" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h1>
<p>Here, we will discuss the <code>DynamicFetcher</code> class (formerly <code>PlayWrightFetcher</code>). This class provides flexible browser automation with multiple configuration options and little under-the-hood stealth improvements.</p>
<p>As we will explain later, to automate the page, you need some knowledge of <a href="https://playwright.dev/python/docs/api/class-page">Playwright's Page API</a>.</p>
<div class="admonition success">
<p class="admonition-title">Prerequisites</p>
<ol>
<li>You've completed or read the <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/choosing.html">Fetchers basics</a> page to understand what the <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/choosing.html#response-object">Response object</a> is and which fetcher to use.</li>
<li>You've completed or read the <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/parsing/selection.html">Querying elements</a> page to understand how to find/extract elements from the <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/parsing/main_classes.html#selector">Selector</a>/<a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/choosing.html#response-object">Response</a> object.</li>
<li>You've completed or read the <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/parsing/main_classes.html">Main classes</a> page to know what properties/methods the <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/choosing.html#response-object">Response</a> class is inheriting from the <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/parsing/main_classes.html#selector">Selector</a> class.</li>
</ol>
</div>
<h2 id="basic-usage">Basic Usage<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#basic-usage" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h2>
<p>You have one primary way to import this Fetcher, which is the same for all fetchers.</p>
<p></p><div class="language-python highlight"><pre id="__code_0"><span></span><nav class="md-code__nav"><button class="md-code__button" title="Copy to clipboard" data-clipboard-target="#__code_0 > code" data-md-type="copy"></button></nav><code class="md-code__content"><span id="__span-0-1"><a id="__codelineno-0-1" name="__codelineno-0-1" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-0-1"></a><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">scrapling.fetchers</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">DynamicFetcher</span>
</span></code></pre></div>
Check out how to configure the parsing options <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/choosing.html#parser-configuration-in-all-fetchers">here</a><p></p>
<p>Now, we will review most of the arguments one by one, using examples. If you want to jump to a table of all arguments for quick reference, <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#full-list-of-arguments">click here</a></p>
<div class="admonition abstract">
<p class="admonition-title">Abstract</p>
<p>The async version of the <code>fetch</code> method is <code>async_fetch</code>, of course.</p>
</div>
<p>This fetcher currently provides three main run options that can be combined as desired.</p>
<p>Which are:</p>
<h3 id="1-vanilla-playwright">1. Vanilla Playwright<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#1-vanilla-playwright" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h3>
<p></p><div class="language-python highlight"><pre id="__code_1"><span></span><nav class="md-code__nav"><button class="md-code__button" title="Copy to clipboard" data-clipboard-target="#__code_1 > code" data-md-type="copy"></button></nav><code class="md-code__content"><span id="__span-1-1"><a id="__codelineno-1-1" name="__codelineno-1-1" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-1-1"></a><span class="n">DynamicFetcher</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">fetch</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">'https://example.com'</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code></pre></div>
Using it in that manner will open a Chromium browser and load the page. There are optimizations for speed, and some stealth goes automatically under the hood, but other than that, there are no tricks or extra features unless you enable some; it's just a plain PlayWright API.<p></p>
<h3 id="2-real-chrome">2. Real Chrome<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#2-real-chrome" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h3>
<p></p><div class="language-python highlight"><pre id="__code_2"><span></span><nav class="md-code__nav"><button class="md-code__button" title="Copy to clipboard" data-clipboard-target="#__code_2 > code" data-md-type="copy"></button></nav><code class="md-code__content"><span id="__span-2-1"><a id="__codelineno-2-1" name="__codelineno-2-1" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-2-1"></a><span class="n">DynamicFetcher</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">fetch</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">'https://example.com'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">real_chrome</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">True</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code></pre></div>
If you have a Google Chrome browser installed, use this option. It's the same as the first option, but it will use the Google Chrome browser you installed on your device instead of Chromium. This will make your requests look more authentic, so they're less detectable for better results.<p></p>
<p>If you don't have Google Chrome installed and want to use this option, you can use the command below in the terminal to install it for the library instead of installing it manually:
</p><div class="language-text highlight"><pre id="__code_3"><span></span><nav class="md-code__nav"><button class="md-code__button" title="Copy to clipboard" data-clipboard-target="#__code_3 > code" data-md-type="copy"></button></nav><code class="md-code__content"><span id="__span-3-1"><a id="__codelineno-3-1" name="__codelineno-3-1" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-3-1"></a>playwright install chrome
</span></code></pre></div><p></p>
<h3 id="3-cdp-connection">3. CDP Connection<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#3-cdp-connection" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h3>
<p></p><div class="language-python highlight"><pre id="__code_4"><span></span><nav class="md-code__nav"><button class="md-code__button" title="Copy to clipboard" data-clipboard-target="#__code_4 > code" data-md-type="copy"></button></nav><code class="md-code__content"><span id="__span-4-1"><a id="__codelineno-4-1" name="__codelineno-4-1" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-4-1"></a><span class="n">DynamicFetcher</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">fetch</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">'https://example.com'</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">cdp_url</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'ws://localhost:9222'</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code></pre></div>
Instead of launching a browser locally (Chromium/Google Chrome), you can connect to a remote browser through the <a href="https://chromedevtools.github.io/devtools-protocol/">Chrome DevTools Protocol</a>.<p></p>
<div class="admonition note">
<p class="admonition-title">Notes:</p>
<ul>
<li>There was a <code>stealth</code> option here, but it was moved to the <code>StealthyFetcher</code> class, as explained on the next page, with additional features since version 0.3.13.<br></li>
<li>This makes it less confusing for new users, easier to maintain, and provides other benefits, as explained on the <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/stealthy.html">StealthyFetcher page</a>.</li>
</ul>
</div>
<h2 id="full-list-of-arguments">Full list of arguments<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#full-list-of-arguments" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h2>
<p>Scrapling provides many options with this fetcher and its session classes. To make it as simple as possible, we will list the options here and give examples of how to use most of them.</p>
<div class="md-typeset__scrollwrap"><div class="md-typeset__table"><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">Argument</th>
<th>Description</th>
<th style="text-align: center;">Optional</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">url</td>
<td>Target url</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">❌</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">headless</td>
<td>Pass <code>True</code> to run the browser in headless/hidden (<strong>default</strong>) or <code>False</code> for headful/visible mode.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">disable_resources</td>
<td>Drop requests for unnecessary resources for a speed boost. Requests dropped are of type <code>font</code>, <code>image</code>, <code>media</code>, <code>beacon</code>, <code>object</code>, <code>imageset</code>, <code>texttrack</code>, <code>websocket</code>, <code>csp_report</code>, and <code>stylesheet</code>.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">cookies</td>
<td>Set cookies for the next request.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">useragent</td>
<td>Pass a useragent string to be used. <strong>Otherwise, the fetcher will generate and use a real Useragent of the same browser and version.</strong></td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">network_idle</td>
<td>Wait for the page until there are no network connections for at least 500 ms.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">load_dom</td>
<td>Enabled by default, wait for all JavaScript on page(s) to fully load and execute (wait for the <code>domcontentloaded</code> state).</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">timeout</td>
<td>The timeout (milliseconds) used in all operations and waits through the page. The default is 30,000 ms (30 seconds).</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">wait</td>
<td>The time (milliseconds) the fetcher will wait after everything finishes before closing the page and returning the <code>Response</code> object.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">page_action</td>
<td>Added for automation. Pass a function that takes the <code>page</code> object, runs after navigation, and does the necessary automation.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">page_setup</td>
<td>A function that takes the <code>page</code> object, runs before navigation. Use it to register event listeners or routes that must be set up before the page loads.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">wait_selector</td>
<td>Wait for a specific css selector to be in a specific state.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">init_script</td>
<td>An absolute path to a JavaScript file to be executed on page creation for all pages in this session.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">wait_selector_state</td>
<td>Scrapling will wait for the given state to be fulfilled for the selector given with <code>wait_selector</code>. <em>Default state is <code>attached</code>.</em></td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">google_search</td>
<td>Enabled by default, Scrapling will set a Google referer header.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">extra_headers</td>
<td>A dictionary of extra headers to add to the request. <em>The referer set by <code>google_search</code> takes priority over the referer set here if used together.</em></td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">proxy</td>
<td>The proxy to be used with requests. It can be a string or a dictionary with only the keys 'server', 'username', and 'password'.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">real_chrome</td>
<td>If you have a Chrome browser installed on your device, enable this, and the Fetcher will launch and use an instance of your browser.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">locale</td>
<td>Specify user locale, for example, <code>en-GB</code>, <code>de-DE</code>, etc. Locale will affect <code>navigator.language</code> value, <code>Accept-Language</code> request header value, as well as number and date formatting rules. Defaults to the system default locale.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">timezone_id</td>
<td>Changes the timezone of the browser. Defaults to the system timezone.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">cdp_url</td>
<td>Instead of launching a new browser instance, connect to this CDP URL to control real browsers through CDP.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">user_data_dir</td>
<td>Path to a User Data Directory, which stores browser session data like cookies and local storage. The default is to create a temporary directory. <strong>Only Works with sessions</strong></td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">extra_flags</td>
<td>A list of additional browser flags to pass to the browser on launch.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">additional_args</td>
<td>Additional arguments to be passed to Playwright's context as additional settings, and they take higher priority than Scrapling's settings.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">selector_config</td>
<td>A dictionary of custom parsing arguments to be used when creating the final <code>Selector</code>/<code>Response</code> class.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">blocked_domains</td>
<td>A set of domain names to block requests to. Subdomains are also matched (e.g., <code>"example.com"</code> blocks <code>"sub.example.com"</code> too).</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">block_ads</td>
<td>Block requests to ~3,500 known ad/tracking domains. Can be combined with <code>blocked_domains</code>.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">dns_over_https</td>
<td>Route DNS queries through Cloudflare's DNS-over-HTTPS to prevent DNS leaks when using proxies.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">proxy_rotator</td>
<td>A <code>ProxyRotator</code> instance for automatic proxy rotation. Cannot be combined with <code>proxy</code>.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">retries</td>
<td>Number of retry attempts for failed requests. Defaults to 3.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">retry_delay</td>
<td>Seconds to wait between retry attempts. Defaults to 1.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">capture_xhr</td>
<td>Pass a regex URL pattern string to capture XHR/fetch requests matching it during page load. Captured responses are available via <code>response.captured_xhr</code>. Defaults to <code>None</code> (disabled).</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">executable_path</td>
<td>Absolute path to a custom browser executable to use instead of the bundled Chromium. Useful for non-standard installations or custom browser builds.</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">✔️</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></div></div>
<p>In session classes, all these arguments can be set globally for the session. Still, you can configure each request individually by passing some of the arguments here that can be configured on the browser tab level like: <code>google_search</code>, <code>timeout</code>, <code>wait</code>, <code>page_action</code>, <code>page_setup</code>, <code>extra_headers</code>, <code>disable_resources</code>, <code>wait_selector</code>, <code>wait_selector_state</code>, <code>network_idle</code>, <code>load_dom</code>, <code>blocked_domains</code>, <code>proxy</code>, and <code>selector_config</code>.</p>
<div class="admonition note">
<p class="admonition-title">Notes:</p>
<ol>
<li>The <code>disable_resources</code> option made requests ~25% faster in my tests for some websites and can help save your proxy usage, but be careful with it, as it can cause some websites to never finish loading.</li>
<li>The <code>google_search</code> argument is enabled by default for all requests, setting the referer to <code>https://www.google.com/</code>. If used together with <code>extra_headers</code>, it takes priority over the referer set there.</li>
<li>Since version 0.3.13, the <code>stealth</code> option has been removed here in favor of the <code>StealthyFetcher</code> class, and the <code>hide_canvas</code> option has been moved to it. The <code>disable_webgl</code> argument has been moved to the <code>StealthyFetcher</code> class and renamed as <code>allow_webgl</code>.</li>
<li>If you didn't set a user agent and enabled headless mode, the fetcher will generate a real user agent for the same browser version and use it. If you didn't set a user agent and didn't enable headless mode, the fetcher will use the browser's default user agent, which is the same as in standard browsers in the latest versions.</li>
</ol>
</div>
<h2 id="session-management">Session Management<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#session-management" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h2>

</span><span id="__span-6-14"><a id="__codelineno-6-14" name="__codelineno-6-14" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-6-14"></a>            <span class="n">session</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">fetch</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">'https://dynamic-content.com'</span><span class="p">)</span>

</span></code></pre></div>
<p>You may have noticed the <code>max_pages</code> argument. This is a new argument that enables the fetcher to create a <strong>rotating pool of Browser tabs</strong>. Instead of using a single tab for all your requests, you set a limit on the maximum number of pages that can be displayed at once. With each request, the library will close all tabs that have finished their task and check if the number of the current tabs is lower than the maximum allowed number of pages/tabs, then:</p>
<ol>
<li>If you are within the allowed range, the fetcher will create a new tab for you, and then all is as normal.</li>
<li>Otherwise, it will keep checking every subsecond if creating a new tab is allowed or not for 60 seconds, then raise <code>TimeoutError</code>. This can happen when the website you are fetching becomes unresponsive.</li>
</ol>
<p>This logic allows for multiple URLs to be fetched at the same time in the same browser, which saves a lot of resources, but most importantly, is so fast :)</p>
<p>In versions 0.3 and 0.3.1, the pool was reusing finished tabs to save more resources/time. That logic proved flawed, as it's nearly impossible to protect pages/tabs from contamination by the previous configuration used in the request before this one.</p>
<h3 id="session-benefits">Session Benefits<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#session-benefits" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Browser reuse</strong>: Much faster subsequent requests by reusing the same browser instance.</li>
<li><strong>Cookie persistence</strong>: Automatic cookie and session state handling as any browser does automatically.</li>
<li><strong>Consistent fingerprint</strong>: Same browser fingerprint across all requests.</li>
<li><strong>Memory efficiency</strong>: Better resource usage compared to launching new browsers with each fetch.</li>
</ul>

<h3 id="browser-automation">Browser Automation<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#browser-automation" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h3>
<p>This is where your knowledge about <a href="https://playwright.dev/python/docs/api/class-page">Playwright's Page API</a> comes into play. The function you pass here takes the page object from Playwright's API, performs the desired action, and then the fetcher continues.</p>
<p>This function is executed immediately after waiting for <code>network_idle</code> (if enabled) and before waiting for the <code>wait_selector</code> argument, allowing it to be used for purposes beyond automation. You can alter the page as you want.</p>

</span><span id="__span-13-2"><a id="__codelineno-13-2" name="__codelineno-13-2" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-13-2"></a>

<h3 id="wait-conditions">Wait Conditions<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#wait-conditions" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h3>
<p></p><div class="language-python highlight"><pre id="__code_15"><span></span><nav class="md-code__nav"><button class="md-code__button" title="Copy to clipboard" data-clipboard-target="#__code_15 > code" data-md-type="copy"></button></nav><code class="md-code__content"><span id="__span-15-1"><a id="__codelineno-15-1" name="__codelineno-15-1" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-15-1"></a><span class="c1"># Wait for the selector</span>
</span><span id="__span-15-2"><a id="__codelineno-15-2" name="__codelineno-15-2" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-15-2"></a><span class="n">page</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">DynamicFetcher</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">fetch</span><span class="p">(</span>
</span><span id="__span-15-3"><a id="__codelineno-15-3" name="__codelineno-15-3" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-15-3"></a>    <span class="s1">'https://quotes.toscrape.com/js-delayed/'</span><span class="p">,</span>
</span><span id="__span-15-4"><a id="__codelineno-15-4" name="__codelineno-15-4" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-15-4"></a>    <span class="n">wait_selector</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'.quote'</span><span class="p">,</span>
</span><span id="__span-15-5"><a id="__codelineno-15-5" name="__codelineno-15-5" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-15-5"></a>    <span class="n">wait_selector_state</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="s1">'visible'</span>
</span><span id="__span-15-6"><a id="__codelineno-15-6" name="__codelineno-15-6" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-15-6"></a><span class="p">)</span>
</span></code></pre></div>
This is the last wait the fetcher will do before returning the response (if enabled). You pass a CSS selector to the <code>wait_selector</code> argument, and the fetcher will wait for the state you passed in the <code>wait_selector_state</code> argument to be fulfilled. If you didn't pass a state, the default would be <code>attached</code>, which means it will wait for the element to be present in the DOM.<p></p>
<p>After that, if <code>load_dom</code> is enabled (the default), the fetcher will check again to see if all JavaScript files are loaded and executed (in the <code>domcontentloaded</code> state) or continue waiting. If you have enabled <code>network_idle</code>, the fetcher will wait for <code>network_idle</code> to be fulfilled again, as explained above.</p>
<p>The states the fetcher can wait for can be any of the following (<a href="https://playwright.dev/python/docs/api/class-page#page-wait-for-selector">source</a>):</p>
<ul>
<li><code>attached</code>: Wait for an element to be present in the DOM.</li>
<li><code>detached</code>: Wait for an element to not be present in the DOM.</li>
<li><code>visible</code>: wait for an element to have a non-empty bounding box and no <code>visibility:hidden</code>. Note that an element without any content or with <code>display:none</code> has an empty bounding box and is not considered visible.</li>
<li><code>hidden</code>: wait for an element to be either detached from the DOM, or have an empty bounding box, or <code>visibility:hidden</code>. This is opposite to the <code>'visible'</code> option.</li>
</ul>
<h3 id="capturing-xhrfetch-requests">Capturing XHR/Fetch Requests<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#capturing-xhrfetch-requests" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h3>
<p>Many SPAs load data through background API calls (XHR/fetch). You can capture these requests by passing a regex URL pattern to <code>capture_xhr</code> at the session level:</p>
<div class="language-python highlight"><pre id="__code_16"><span></span><nav class="md-code__nav"><button class="md-code__button" title="Copy to clipboard" data-clipboard-target="#__code_16 > code" data-md-type="copy"></button></nav><code class="md-code__content" tabindex="0"><span id="__span-16-1"><a id="__codelineno-16-1" name="__codelineno-16-1" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-16-1"></a><span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">scrapling.fetchers</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">DynamicSession</span>
</span><span id="__span-16-2"><a id="__codelineno-16-2" name="__codelineno-16-2" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-16-2"></a>
</span><span id="__span-16-3"><a id="__codelineno-16-3" name="__codelineno-16-3" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-16-3"></a><span class="k">with</span> <span class="n">DynamicSession</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">capture_xhr</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="sa">r</span><span class="s2">"https://api\.example\.com/.*"</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">headless</span><span class="o">=</span><span class="kc">True</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="k">as</span> <span class="n">session</span><span class="p">:</span>
</span><span id="__span-16-4"><a id="__codelineno-16-4" name="__codelineno-16-4" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-16-4"></a>    <span class="n">page</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">session</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">fetch</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="s1">'https://example.com'</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="__span-16-5"><a id="__codelineno-16-5" name="__codelineno-16-5" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-16-5"></a>
</span><span id="__span-16-6"><a id="__codelineno-16-6" name="__codelineno-16-6" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-16-6"></a>    <span class="c1"># Access captured XHR responses</span>
</span><span id="__span-16-7"><a id="__codelineno-16-7" name="__codelineno-16-7" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-16-7"></a>    <span class="k">for</span> <span class="n">xhr</span> <span class="ow">in</span> <span class="n">page</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">captured_xhr</span><span class="p">:</span>
</span><span id="__span-16-8"><a id="__codelineno-16-8" name="__codelineno-16-8" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-16-8"></a>        <span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">xhr</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">url</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">xhr</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">status</span><span class="p">)</span>
</span><span id="__span-16-9"><a id="__codelineno-16-9" name="__codelineno-16-9" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#__codelineno-16-9"></a>        <span class="nb">print</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">xhr</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">body</span><span class="p">)</span>  <span class="c1"># Raw response body as bytes</span>
</span></code></pre></div>
<p>Each item in <code>captured_xhr</code> is a full <code>Response</code> object with the same properties (<code>.url</code>, <code>.status</code>, <code>.headers</code>, <code>.body</code>, etc.). When <code>capture_xhr</code> is not set or is <code>None</code>, <code>captured_xhr</code> is an empty list.</p>

<div class="admonition warning">
<p class="admonition-title">Warning</p>
<p>Remember that by default, all browser-based fetchers and sessions use a persistent browser context with a pool of tabs. However, since browsers can't set a proxy per tab, when you use a <code>ProxyRotator</code>, the fetcher will automatically open a separate context for each proxy, with one tab per context. Once the tab's job is done, both the tab and its context are closed.</p>
</div>
<h2 id="when-to-use">When to Use<a class="headerlink" href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/dynamic.html#when-to-use" title="Permanent link">¶</a></h2>
<p>Use DynamicFetcher when:</p>
<ul>
<li>Need browser automation</li>
<li>Want multiple browser options</li>
<li>Using a real Chrome browser</li>
<li>Need custom browser config</li>
<li>Want a few stealth options </li>
</ul>
<p>If you want more stealth and control without much config, check out the <a href="https://scrapling.readthedocs.io/en/latest/fetching/stealthy.html" class="link-preview">StealthyFetcher</a>.</p>
